# Questions in Livestream 180

Live Stream #180: Quirky Square Dancing Publishing

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:58</a> | Was Moses 59 years old at the time of the Exodus, and is the upcoming Exodus book using realistic ages? | He says the book will distinguish realistic ages from idealized or religiously significant numbers and discuss both. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:51</a> | Are angels more like natural forces, animalistic instincts, or hyper-rational creatures? | He says they are a bit of all of these, though less like impersonal natural forces than untamable spirit beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1007" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:47</a> | Can angels who make a bad choice still be saved? | He says no, because angelic rebellion happens with full knowledge rather than under human-style deception. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1218" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:18</a> | Is the Duat a temporary place or an eternal place for the ka and ba? | He says the Duat is the eternal abode of the dead, where the blessed dead work in the fields of Osiris after judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:35</a> | What did the Egyptian king do in the afterlife? | He says the king served as a scribe aboard the bark of Re rather than laboring like ordinary dead people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1439" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:59</a> | What are examples that show the Old Testament cannot simply be read plainly and naively? | He points to cultural context, translation issues, retrojections such as Pharaoh and Rameses, and the need to read from the author's context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:48</a> | What is an archangel? | He says an archangel is a high-ranking angelic being, though the exact kind of spirit creature is not clear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1953" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:33</a> | Teaching first and second graders from Moses' birth to the crossing of the sea in one lesson, what should not be skipped? | He recommends covering Moses in the reeds, Midian and the burning bush, the plagues, the Exodus route through the Reed Sea, and the Pharaoh's identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=2280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:00</a> | How should "be fruitful and multiply" be applied today? | He treats it as a broad creation principle tied to filling and subduing the earth, not simply a narrow literal command in every case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=2487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:27</a> | What if someone is "just" a low-IQ laborer? | He says honest labor done for the glory of God still images God; the issue is whom the labor is for, not the status of the work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=2691" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:51</a> | Are there issues with the verbiage of the Nicene Creed? | He says he has concerns about the Latin descent-into-hell wording and procession/Eternal Generation language, though he can read some wording in an acceptable sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=2879" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:59</a> | What are his thoughts on Dr. Suju's model of the Trinity? | He describes aspectual monarchianism as looking like "modalism light" and says he was not persuaded by the paper. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=3089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:29</a> | If only the Father is God and the Son and Spirit are divine but not fundamentally God, why do Peter and Paul call Jesus God? | He says that view is contradicted by Scripture and makes a category error about the three persons being the one God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=3398" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:38</a> | How intense does heresy have to be before someone is no longer a Christian? | He says this is debated, but heresy involves denial rather than a mere aberration or mistake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=3649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:49</a> | Why do men dominate online theological discussions but make up less of in-person church gatherings? | He attributes it to different demographics, with online discussions skewing younger and male while in-person churches often skew older, and says many online discussants do not attend church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4071" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:51</a> | Did Ethiopians speak Greek? | He says most did not; diplomats might use Greek, but most Ethiopians spoke a Hamito-Semitic, probably Kushite-related, dialect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:56</a> | Is trichinosis from the Americas? | He says its distribution is mainly in the Americas, with another patch in Southeast Asia. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4710" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:30</a> | How do we know Tarshish is Spain and not Tarsus? | He leans toward Spain, mainly because the texts frame Tarshish as a place reached by sea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:43</a> | Does he enjoy "ruining the Bible" for people by explaining what favorite verses actually mean? | He says no; the goal is to educate gently so people mature in the faith and understand the Bible better. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=5253" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:33</a> | If Moses' Exodus age includes a plus-or-minus seven-year range, would it be 48 to 66 rather than 55 to 59? | He agrees that 48 to 66 is more accurate with the error bars; the earlier number was based on the 1265 date without that range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=5295" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:15</a> | If Moses wrote Exodus, why did he not specify the years of his departure and return? | He says Exodus focuses on Israel's relationship with God and Moses' work, while Bronze Age biographies commonly omit ages and early-life chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=5656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:16</a> | Did the apostles perform magic besides divination? | He says they performed miracles, and the one clear divinatory act was casting lots to choose Matthias. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=5970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:30</a> | What makes black magic black? | He says black and white magic are pagan categories, and the Bible rejects both as legitimate spiritual practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:34</a> | Is a protective spell such as "Expecto Patronum" also frowned upon? | He says protective magic would still be frowned on, but his bigger concern with Harry Potter is its situational ethics rather than the fictional magic system itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:35</a> | If word order does not affect meaning in Greek, can it still add emphasis? | He says fronting can add emphasis in some cases, but not every word-order variation carries special meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:57</a> | Why do scholars often describe Revelation as John's literary commentary rather than an actual revelation? | He says that is a false either-or, since Revelation can both unveil and interpret older prophecy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:54</a> | Can he recommend a book, resource, or author on Babylon? | He mentions Alan Millard as someone he often used, while cautioning that Assyriology is not his field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6870" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:30</a> | Did the Greeks have papyrus? | Yes. He says papyrus was an Egyptian export that Greeks acquired through Mediterranean trade. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6943" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:43</a> | With a clan interpretation in mind, were there more than eight people on the ark? | He says the Bible gives at least eight and does not clearly exclude more, but it also does not say there were more. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7217" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:17</a> | Was Satan an angel of music in Isaiah 14:11? | He says we do not know; the passage first concerns the king of Babylon and then shifts, so the music imagery may or may not apply to Satan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7627" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:07</a> | Is the Ham-and-stepmother reading of Noah's story rabbinical in origin or from other sources? | He says he gets it from reading the text in context, not from rabbinical sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7671" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:51</a> | What is the scriptural evidence for spirit creatures? | He points to angels, prophetic visions, Eden, and other biblical passages where spirit beings appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:53</a> | Are chaos monsters also spirit creatures? | Yes. He treats them as part of the spiritual side effects built into ancient cosmological thinking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:16</a> | If God knows what Satan will do and how he will stop him, why does Satan still go through with it? | He says Satan is a defeated enemy trying to delay the inevitable until the fullness of the Gentiles is complete. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:55</a> | What do you make of skeptics who say God should not judge the world or Sodom, but should have stopped Satan or Hitler? | He says that standard is inconsistent because it criticizes divine judgment and demands it at the same time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:38</a> | When praying, should someone try to look better for God or come in a humbler, less flattering way? | He treats this as morally neutral and says God cares more that a person prays than how they look while praying. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:10</a> | Is Jesus the Son of God or a prophet? | He says both: Jesus is the Son of God, God incarnate, fully God and fully man, and also a prophet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=9102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:42</a> | Why do skeptics say that if God is good he should cure diseases and end crime, and why would God create or need us? | He says God does not need humans, and ending all evil by force would remove free will; God allows larger goods and human work to unfold. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=9765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:45</a> | Why did God punish David's infant for David's crime? | He says God punished David rather than the infant, describing the death as God removing protection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=10234" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:34</a> | Does high child mortality help select for good genes? | He says no; high child mortality was largely driven by infection and killed indiscriminately rather than selecting for stronger genes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=10542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:42</a> | Is Dan McClellan a good Bible scholar, and are there good secular skeptical Bible scholars? | He says he does not think McClellan is good because he does not move the conversation forward, but he names William Propp and Margaret Barker as stronger examples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=10982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:02</a> | Is Margaret Barker's temple theory a problem for Old Testament theology? | He says no and treats temple theology as an important interpretive frame, especially for Genesis 1. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=11070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:30</a> | In which Hammurabi year did the Teru correspondence happen? | He says the Teru and Zimri-Lim correspondence predates Hammurabi's conquest of Zimri-Lim, which he places in Hammurabi's 35th regnal year. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:32</a> | Was David's kingdom exaggerated in power? | He says probably not, though Solomon's kingdom is the bigger question; David was still fighting Philistine wars. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:52</a> | How did Egyptians handle children with disabilities? | He says they sometimes provided aids such as crutches, litters, or limited sign communication depending on the disability and status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12560" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:20</a> | What about Margaret Barker's theory that Torah was polytheistic until later scribes changed it? | He rejects that claim, saying the people were polytheistic but he does not see evidence that Torah itself was polytheistic or later changed that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12732" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:12</a> | Is a new book coming soon? | He says the Exodus plagues proposal is close, and that they also have two fiction novels ready with a third in progress. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:08</a> | Did Michael Heiser write fiction? | Yes. He says Heiser used fiction as one way of engaging the culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=13685" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:05</a> | Did Daniel have magic dishes that he used in ritual? | He says we are not sure; Joseph had a divination cup, and such ritual dishes existed, but Daniel's use is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=13754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:14</a> | Why do people call the six-pointed star Solomon's star or the Star of David? | He says it became associated with Judaism later, with the earliest example he cites from Bar Kokhba revolt coinage, not early Israelite religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=13864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:04</a> | If Daniel was called captain of the Babylonian Magi, does that mean he practiced magic? | He says not necessarily; Babylonian Magi were partly scholars, though Babylonian practice included codified divination such as dream interpretation and entrail reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=14333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:53</a> | Who were the Ophites, and how did ancient Christians relate to them? | He describes them as Jewish and later Christian gnostics who elevated the serpent as a giver of salvific knowledge; tension arose when gnostic writings confused Christians. |
